details.text: Derivative: الضُّحَىٰ refers to the morning after sunset which is the time of day which is full of activity and movement.

Derivative: تَنْهَرْ means to scold, rebuke or drive away. Here it means to deal or to speak in a harsh manner. The linkage to نَهْر (a river) is that when you scold and rebuke somebody it is like they are in a river and being struck repeatedly by its strong currents.
Derivative: نِعْمَةِ means favour or blessing. It comes from the original root meaning of ease, comfort and softness.
بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ – the بِ here implies the meaning of because i.e., because of the favour of your Rabb.
Derivative: سَجَىٰ means to become still and calm. Many scholars of Tafsir have also said that it means to be covered in darkness.
Derivative: وَدَّعَ – يُوَدِّعُ – تَوْدِيع means to bid farewell and implies here to abandon or forsake. تَوْدِيع is only used amongst those who have warm and loving relations.
Derivative: قَلَىٰ means to be angry and refers here to cutting off communication with someone due to being angry or displeased with him.

مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ

- It can be observed that with وَدَّعَ (bid farewell) Allah mentioned whom He did not bid farewell to by using the pronoun كَ (you), however when mentioning His displeasure or anger with the word قَلَىٰ, He did not attach a pronoun as the object of the verb (المَفْعول بِهِ), which means he did not directly attach the word to be angry and displeased with His Messenger ﷺ even though that would be usual in the Arabic language. This is indicative of Allah’s honouring His Messenger ﷺ by not associating His anger or displeasure with him. The omission of the object (المَفْعول بِهِ) of the verb can also be a style that leaves multiple possible meanings open. For instance, the object of the negation of Allah’s displeasure or anger could be the Messenger ﷺ in addition to his companions and his mission.
- The use of مَا with the past tense وَدَّعَ makes the statement more emphatic i.e., “He did not forsake you at all”.

وَلَلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لَكَ مِنَ الْأُولَىٰ

- “And truly the last (لَلْآخِرَةُ) is better for you (خَيْرٌ لَكَ) than the first (مِنَ الْأُولَىٰ)”. The last and first have been understood to refer to the hereafter and the life of this world. They have also been interpreted to refer to the first part of the mission and Prophetic life of the Messenger ﷺ and to the last part of his mission and Prophetic life. The two meanings can be brought together by the meaning that “your future will be better” or “what lies in store for you is better”.

- The لَام (Laam – لَ) attached to الْآخِرَةُ (Al-Aakhirah - The hereafter) is لَام التَوْكِيد (The Laam of emphasis) and makes the meaning: “Truly or indeed, the hereafter is …”. It is used to quash doubts.
Derivative: يُعْطِي from أَعْطَى – يُعْطِي – إِعطاء means to give a lot and it refers to the type of giving that has no strings or conditions attached.

Derivative: تَرْضَىٰ is from رَضِيَ – يَرْضَى – رِضَاء and carries the meanings of to be pleased, contented and satisfied. In the Qur’an the attainment of رِضَاء (contentment and satisfaction) is used in connection to Jannah.

Derivative: سَوْفَ and سَ can be attached to the present tense verb (المُضارِع) to indicate to the future with the meaning of "will", like in the statement: “I will do ...”. سَوْفَ is used when the time in the future being indicated to is further away and سَ when it is closer.

وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضَىٰ

- “And your Rabb (رَبُّكَ) will surely (لَسَوْفَ) give you (يُعْطِيكَ), so/then you will be pleased (فَتَرْضَىٰ)” – In this statement it is not stated what will be given in terms of the object of the verb (المَفْعُولُ بَهِ). It has therefore been left unspecified, absolute and unrestricted which adds to the meaning of يُعْطِيكَ “He will give you” in the case where أَعْطَى – يُعْطِي – إِعطاء implies to give a lot.
- Similarly, فَتَرْضَىٰ “Then you will be pleased” does not mention the object of the pleasure i.e., who are what you will be pleased with. It has also been left unspecified and unrestricted. It can therefore be understood to imply that he will be pleased with Allah and also pleased with all that Allah will bestow upon Him.
Derivative: آوَىٰ – يُؤْوِي – إيْواء means to provide someone with shelter or accommodate someone in one’s home and take him as a guest.

Derivative: ضَالًّا means to be lost or lost one’s way from ضَلَّ – يَضِلُّ – ضَلَال or ضَلَالَة which means to lose one’s way, to stray or go astray. ضَالًّا (being lost) also implies that you are seeking something i.e., a way out from being lost.

وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا فَهَدَىٰ

- “And He found you (وَوَجَدَكَ) lost (ضَالًّا), then He guided (فَهَدَىٰ)” – فَهَدَىٰ “Then He guided” was left open and unspecified. Therefore, in addition to meaning “Then He guided you” it is open to include other meanings such as “Then He guided (others) by (means of) you” or “Then, He guided for you”.
Derivative: أَغْنَىٰ means to make somebody free of need, self-sufficient or to enrich.

Derivative: عَائِلًا is the اِسْمُ الفَاعِل (noun of the doer) from عَالَ – يَعِيلُ – عَيْلَة which means to be or become poor or to be reduced to poverty. It can imply somebody who is under a lot of pressure and whose burden needs to be alleviated.

وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلًا فَأَغْنَىٰ

- “And He found you (وَوَجَدَكَ) poor (عَائِلًا), then He made self-sufficient (فَأَغْنَىٰ)”. Allah does not mention who He enriched or how He enriched but rather leaves it open and unspecified. It can therefore mean “Then, He made you self-sufficient or free of need” but is also open to other additional meanings such as “The, He made you free of need for you” or “He made others free of need through you”. By taking away specification the language provides expansion in terms of meanings that can be derived.
Derivative: تَقْهَرْ it means to be overpowering in a manner that is oppressive and humiliating. It can imply in this case to being harsh and mean in an overbearing manner.
